title: 10 best herbal teas for chickenpox
intro_desc: Herbal teas for chickenpox are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and roots, which can help alleviate symptoms of chickenpox such as fever, itching, and irritability. Chamomile tea, for instance, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. Licorice root tea can help soothe a sore throat and boost the immune system, while peppermint tea can help relieve itching and reduce fever. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals with chickenpox can experience improved sleep, reduced discomfort, and faster healing. These teas offer a natural and gentle alternative to over-the-counter medications, making them an excellent addition to any chickenpox treatment plan.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with chickenpox because it contains mucilage, a substance that forms a soothing film over mucous membranes. This film can help relieve the itching and inflammation associated with chickenpox. Additionally, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of the illness. The tea is also caffeine-free and hydrating, making it a gentle and comforting choice for children and adults dealing with the discomfort of chickenpox.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to its mucilage content, can help alleviate the itching and swelling associated with chickenpox blisters.
Demulcent: The soothing demulcent properties of marshmallow tea, caused by its mucilage content, can help reduce irritation and promote healing of the skin affected by chickenpox.
Immune-boosting: Marshmallow tea's ability to boost the immune system, due to its antioxidant compounds, can help the body fight off the chickenpox virus more effectively.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of marshmallow tea, attributed to its tannins and flavonoids, can help protect the skin from secondary bacterial infections during the chickenpox healing process.

Diaphoretic: Marshmallow tea's diaphoretic properties, resulting from its various plant compounds, can help regulate body temperature and support the body's natural response to fever, a common symptom of chickenpox.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antiviral compounds, marshmallow leaves help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation caused by chickenpox.
Flowers: Containing mucilage, marshmallow flowers help relieve itchiness and promote faster healing of chickenpox blisters.
Roots: High in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, marshmallow roots aid in reducing redness and preventing infection in chickenpox lesions.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 quart of water, 2 tablespoons of dried chamomile flowers, and 2 tablespoons of dried marshmallow root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove solids, then add 1-2 tablespoons of honey for added flavor and soothing properties.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Serve the tea in 8 ounce portions, up to three times daily, during the duration of chickenpox symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with chickenpox because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can provide relief from the itching and inflammation caused by the virus. The tea is rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have antiviral effects, potentially helping to reduce the severity and duration of the infection. Additionally, pot marigold tea is a natural source of vitamin C, which can help to boost the immune system and speed up the healing process. Drinking pot marigold tea can also help to keep the body hydrated, which is important for overall health and can help to alleviate some of the symptoms associated with chickenpox.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with chickenpox blisters, thanks to the anti-inflammatory properties of flavonoids and triterpenes.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of pot marigold, due to the presence of essential oils and tannins, can help prevent secondary infections during chickenpox.
Immune-boosting: Pot marigold tea can help enhance the immune system, allowing the body to fight off the virus more effectively, due to the presence of antioxidants like flavonoids and carotenoids.

Diaphoretic: Pot marigold tea can promote sweating, which helps the body eliminate toxins and reduce fever, a common symptom of chickenpox, thanks to its diaphoretic properties caused by the plant's essential oils and flavonoids.
Wound-healing: The wound-healing properties of pot marigold, due to the presence of triterpenes and flavonoids, can help soothe and heal chickenpox blisters more quickly.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing chickenpox symptoms.
Leaves: Contain high levels of flavonoids and carotenoids, which help boost the immune system and promote faster healing.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants and essential oils, they help alleviate itching and inflammation caused by chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried pot marigold flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and strain the tea into a cup, discarding the flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Have the person with chickenpox drink the entire serving of tea, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with chickenpox because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can alleviate the symptoms of this viral infection. The tea's anti-inflammatory agents can soothe the itchiness and irritation of the skin lesions, providing relief to the affected individual. Moreover, its antimicrobial properties can help prevent secondary bacterial infections that may occur due to scratching. Furthermore, chamomile tea contains compounds that have a calming effect, which can help reduce anxiety and promote better sleep, essential for a quick recovery. However, it is always advisable to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially during pregnancy, breastfeeding, or when taking other medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains active constituents like alpha-bisabolol and matricin, which help reduce inflammation and provide relief from itching and irritation caused by chickenpox blisters.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of German chamomile tea, due to its chamazulene content, can help prevent secondary bacterial infections that may occur as a result of scratching or open sores from chickenpox.
Antioxidant: German chamomile tea contains flavonoids and terpenoids that exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help boost the immune system and promote faster healing of chickenpox lesions.

Sedative: German chamomile tea contains apigenin, a flavonoid with sedative properties, which can help reduce anxiety and promote better sleep, both of which are essential for a quicker recovery from chickenpox.

Diaphoretic: German chamomile tea helps stimulate perspiration, which can aid in detoxification and elimination of toxins from the body, thereby supporting the immune system and contributing to a faster recovery from chickenpox.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, chamomile flowers help soothe skin irritations and reduce redness caused by chickenpox.
Leaves: Containing antiviral and antimicrobial properties, chamomile leaves support the immune system and aid in faster recovery from chickenpox.
Seeds: High in essential oils, chamomile seeds provide calming effects and help alleviate itching and discomfort associated with chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent flowers.
Serve the hot tea to the person with chickenpox, recommending them to drink 2-3 cups per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with chickenpox because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and irritation of the skin caused by the virus. Additionally, horsetail is rich in silica, a mineral that can help boost the immune system and promote healing, which is essential for recovering from chickenpox. The antiviral properties of horsetail tea may also help to inhibit the spread of the virus, providing relief from symptoms such as itching and blistering. Furthermore, horsetail tea can help to detoxify the body, which is beneficial for eliminating toxins and promoting overall health and well-being during the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox blisters, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause secondary infections in chickenpox lesions, due to the presence of phenolic acids and tannins.
Astringent: Horsetail tea's astringent properties help dry out chickenpox blisters and speed up the healing process, as a result of tannins that constrict blood vessels and tighten skin tissue.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of horsetail tea support the immune system, which is crucial for fighting off the chickenpox virus, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Diuretic: Horsetail tea's diuretic properties help eliminate toxins from the body, reducing the severity and duration of chickenpox symptoms, thanks to the presence of equisetin and other saponins.
remedy_parts: Stems: The stems of horsetail tea plant contain high levels of silica, which can help to dry out and heal chickenpox blisters.
Leaves: The leaves of horsetail tea plant are rich in minerals, which can help to boost the immune system and speed up the healing process for chickenpox.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of horsetail tea plant contain anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with chickenpox.
Flowers: The flowers of horsetail tea plant have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote healing for chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 quart of water and 2 tablespoons of dried horsetail herb, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup or mug, discarding the used horsetail herb.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Serve the herbal horsetail tea to the individual with chickenpox, recommending them to drink 2-3 cups per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Meadowsweet tea helps with chickenpox because it contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. This compound has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and itching associated with chickenpox. Additionally, meadowsweet tea has been traditionally used for its antipyretic effects, which can help reduce fever. The soothing and calming nature of the tea can also promote rest and relaxation, aiding in a quicker recovery. However, it is always important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedies, especially for children.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Meadowsweet tea contains salicylates, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate itching associated with chickenpox blisters.
Fever-reducing: The salicylates in meadowsweet tea act as a natural analgesic and antipyretic, helping to reduce fever and discomfort caused by chickenpox.
Diaphoretic: Meadowsweet tea promotes perspiration, which helps the body eliminate toxins and reduce fever during chickenpox.
Astringent: The tannins present in meadowsweet tea have an astringent effect, helping to dry out chickenpox blisters and speed up the healing process.
Vitamin-rich: Meadowsweet tea contains vitamins A, C, and E, which support the immune system and promote skin healing during chickenpox.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in salicylic acid, the flowers are used to reduce fever and inflammation associated with chickenpox.
Leaves: Containing tannins and flavonoids, the leaves help soothe skin irritation and itching caused by chickenpox.
Stems: High in essential oils, the stems add a calming aroma to the tea, aiding in relaxation during chickenpox recovery.
Rhyzomes: Packed with antioxidants, rhyzomes help boost the immune system and speed up recovery from chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried meadowsweet herb for every 8 ounces of water.
Pour boiling water over the herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs, leaving only the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down to a safe temperature for children.
Serve the tea to the person with chickenpox, recommending 3 cups per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with chickenpox because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These properties can help reduce the severity and duration of chickenpox symptoms, such as itching, inflammation, and fever. Additionally, licorice tea can help soothe a sore throat and ease coughing, which are common symptoms associated with chickenpox. The tea's natural ingredients can also help boost the immune system, allowing the body to fight off the virus more effectively. However, it is important to note that licorice tea should not be given to children under two years old, and pregnant or breastfeeding women should consult with a healthcare provider before consuming licorice tea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to active constituents like glycyrrhizin, help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox blisters.
Antiviral: Licorice tea's antiviral properties, from compounds like flavonoids, can help inhibit the spread of the varicella-zoster virus that causes chickenpox.
Demulcent: The soothing and protective qualities of licorice tea, resulting from its mucilage content, help ease irritation and discomfort on the skin caused by chickenpox.
Immune-boosting: Licorice tea's ability to support the immune system, thanks to active components like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, can help the body combat and recover from chickenpox.
Antipruritic: Licorice tea's antipruritic properties, derived from glycyrrhizin, can help alleviate itchiness, a common and distressing symptom of chickenpox.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tea plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the itching and discomfort associated with chickenpox.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tea plant contain compounds that can help boost the immune system and speed up the healing process during chickenpox.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice tea plant are rich in antioxidants that can help protect the body from the harmful effects of the chickenpox virus.
Bark: The bark of the licorice tea plant has been shown to have antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of chickenpox symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried licorice root, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat, add 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers, and let steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea, then add honey to taste.
Serve the tea warm, up to three times a day, for a maximum of 5 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus tea, also known as hops tea, helps with chickenpox because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The herb contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation and swelling, providing relief from the itchy and painful blisters caused by the chickenpox virus. Additionally, hops have been shown to have antiviral effects, potentially helping to inhibit the spread of the virus and reduce the severity of the infection. Drinking humulus lupulus tea may help soothe symptoms and promote a faster recovery from chickenpox. However, it is important to note that while herbal remedies can provide relief from symptoms, they should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment and advice.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Humulus lupulus (hops) tea can help reduce inflammation and itching caused by chickenpox blisters, thanks to its anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and essential oils.
Antimicrobial: Hops tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent secondary bacterial infections in chickenpox lesions, due to the presence of humulones and lupulones.
Antipyretic: Humulus lupulus tea can help lower fever associated with chickenpox by acting as a natural antipyretic, which is attributed to its essential oils and bitter acids.

Sedative: Hops tea has a calming effect that can promote better sleep and help alleviate irritability and restlessness caused by chickenpox, attributed to its sedative constituents like dimethylvinyl carbinol and methylbutenol.

Diaphoretic: Humulus lupulus tea can induce perspiration, which aids in the elimination of toxins from the body and helps cool down the body, providing relief from chickenpox symptoms; this is due to the presence of volatile oils and tannins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity of chickenpox symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that can alleviate skin irritation and itching caused by chickenpox.
Buds: High in antioxidants that can support the immune system and promote faster recovery.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried Humulus lupulus (hops) flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat and allow the tea to steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the hops flowers, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Serve the herbal Humulus lupulus tea to the person with chickenpox, recommending them to drink 2-3 cups per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tea helps with chickenpox because it has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can alleviate the symptoms of the virus. The herbal tea contains hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and inhibit the replication of the virus that causes chickenpox. Drinking St. John's wort tea may help soothe itchy skin, reduce redness and swelling, and promote faster healing of the blisters caused by the virus. Additionally, the tea's antiviral properties may help shorten the duration of the illness and prevent complications. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before taking St. John's wort tea or any other herbal remedy for chickenpox.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: St. John's wort tea has antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of the chickenpox virus (Varicella-zoster) infection. The active constituents responsible for these antiviral effects are hypericin and hyperforin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea can help alleviate skin inflammation and itching caused by chickenpox lesions, thanks to its anti-inflammatory compounds like hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Analgesic properties: St. John's wort tea can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with chickenpox due to its analgesic properties. Hypericin and hyperforin contribute to this pain-relieving effect.

Immunomodulatory properties: The tea can help regulate and support the immune system, which is crucial for fighting off the chickenpox virus. Hyperforin and flavonoids are responsible for the tea's immunomodulatory effects.

Wound-healing properties: St. John's wort tea promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can help speed up the recovery process of chickenpox-related skin lesions. The active constituents responsible for these wound-healing properties include hypericin, hyperforin, and tannins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of St. John's Wort are used in medicinal tea for chickenpox due to their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate skin irritation and speed up healing.

Leaves: The leaves of St. John's Wort contain active compounds that have been shown to have antiviral and pain-relieving effects, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea for treating chickenpox symptoms.

Stems: Although less potent than the flowers and leaves, the stems of St. John's Wort still contain medicinal properties and are often included in tea blends to provide structure and additional benefits for treating chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried St. John's Wort flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the herb's properties.
Strain the tea to remove the spent flowers and retain the liquid.
For children, give 1-2 teaspoons of the tea every few hours; for adults, drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea up to three times a day.
Consume the tea while it's still warm, and continue the treatment for a few days or until the chickenpox rash improves.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with chickenpox because it has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the itchiness and discomfort caused by the virus. The lavender oil found in the tea contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and provide pain relief. Additionally, lavender tea can help promote relaxation and rest, which is crucial for a speedy recovery. Drinking lavender tea can also boost the immune system, helping the body fight off the virus more effectively. Overall, English lavender tea can provide much-needed relief and support for those suffering from chickenpox.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: English lavender tea has antimicrobial properties that help prevent secondary bacterial infections in chickenpox lesions, due to its active constituents like linalool and eucalyptol.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of lavender tea help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with chickenpox blisters, thanks to compounds like cineole and camphor.
Analgesic properties: English lavender tea contains analgesic constituents, such as linalyl acetate and lavandulol, which help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by chickenpox.
Anxiolytic properties: Lavender tea has anxiolytic effects, reducing anxiety and promoting better sleep, which is crucial during chickenpox recovery, due to its active compound linalool.
Antioxidant properties: English lavender tea's antioxidant properties support the immune system and aid in wound healing, as it contains antioxidants like rosmarinic acid and apigenin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, English lavender flowers help soothe skin irritation and promote healing in chickenpox.
Leaves: Containing therapeutic oils, lavender leaves can alleviate itching and reduce inflammation caused by chickenpox.
Buds: High in antimicrobial properties, lavender buds can help prevent infection and speed up recovery from chickenpox.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried English lavender flowers, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 15 minutes, and allow the lavender flowers to infuse the water.
Strain the tea to remove the lavender flowers, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Stir in 1-2 teaspoons of raw honey for added flavor and soothing properties.
Serve the herbal English lavender tea warm, and encourage the person with chickenpox to drink 2-3 cups per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with chickenpox because it has antiviral properties that may help reduce the severity and duration of the virus. It contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and flavonoids that can inhibit the activity of the virus responsible for causing chickenpox. Additionally, lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects that can help alleviate the itching, redness, and swelling associated with chickenpox. Drinking lemon balm tea can also boost the immune system, helping the body fight off the virus more effectively. Furthermore, it has a calming effect that can promote rest and relaxation, which is essential for a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have antiviral effects, helping to reduce the severity and duration of chickenpox by inhibiting the ability of the virus to replicate.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm tea contains eugenol, which has anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate itching, swelling, and redness associated with chickenpox.

Sedative properties: Lemon balm tea contains citronellal, which has sedative effects, helping to promote rest and relaxation, which is important for healing and reducing stress in individuals with chickenpox.

Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which have antioxidant effects, helping to protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by the chickenpox virus.
Immune-boosting properties: Lemon balm tea contains various polyphenols and flavonoids, which have immune-boosting effects, helping to strengthen the body's defenses against the chickenpox virus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant have antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity of chickenpox symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant have a calming effect and can help reduce fever and inflammation associated with chickenpox.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant contain tannins that can help dry out chickenpox blisters and speed up the healing process.
remedy_recipe: Pick 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or 1 tablespoon of dried leaves and rinse them under cold water.
Boil 1 cup of water and add the lemon balm leaves, then let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves and allow it to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey for added flavor and soothing properties (optional).
Serve the tea to the person with chickenpox, recommending 3 cups per day for a week.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chickenpox with herbal teas is by consuming chamomile tea. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the itchiness and discomfort caused by chickenpox. Drinking peppermint tea can also provide relief as it has cooling and calming effects on the skin. Additionally, lemon balm tea can boost the immune system, helping the body fight off the virus. However, it's important to note that while herbal teas can help alleviate symptoms, they should not replace medical treatment. Always consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chickenpox that are treated with herbal teas include measles and shingles. For measles, teas made from herbs such as baical skullcap, chamomile, and liquorice root can help soothe symptoms and boost the immune system. Meanwhile, for shingles, teas made from St. John's Wort, valerian root, and passionflower can help reduce pain and inflammation, as well as promote healing. However, it's important to note that while herbal teas can be helpful in managing symptoms, they should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment. Always consult with a healthcare provider for proper diagnosis and treatment.
